author: Frank Herbert
title: Dune
description: A complex novel of politics, religion, and ecology set on the desert planet Arrakis.

author: J.K. Rowling
title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
description: An orphaned boy discovers he is a wizard and attends a magical academy.

author: J.R.R. Tolkien
title: The Hobbit
description: A reluctant hobbit leaves his home to help a group of dwarves reclaim their mountain from a dragon.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society under constant surveillance by Big Brother.

author: Ray Bradbury
title: Fahrenheit 451
description: In a future where books are banned, a fireman tasked with burning them begins to question his life.

author: Aldous Huxley
title: Brave New World
description: A dark vision of a future society driven by technological control, conditioning, and soma.

author: Douglas Adams
title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: An Earthman's comedic journey through space after Earth is demolished for a hyperspace bypass.

author: Isaac Asimov
title: Foundation
description: A mathematician develops psychohistory to predict and save humanity from a galactic dark age.

author: Orson Scott Card
title: Ender's Game
description: A gifted child is sent to an advanced military academy in space to prepare for an alien invasion.

author: William Gibson
title: Neuromancer
description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired by a mysterious employer for the ultimate hack.

author: Harper Lee
title: To Kill a Mockingbird
description: A young girl in the depression-era South learns about racism and injustice through her lawyer father.

author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
title: The Great Gatsby
description: A tragic love story exploring the decadence and illusions of the Jazz Age in 1920s America.

author: Jane Austen
title: Pride and Prejudice
description: A classic romance novel dealing with issues of class, marriage, and manners in early 19th-century England.

author: J.D. Salinger
title: The Catcher in the Rye
description: The experiences of an alienated teenager, Holden Caulfield, navigating post-WWII New York.

author: Herman Melville
title: Moby-Dick
description: Captain Ahab's obsessive quest for revenge against the great white whale.

author: Charlotte Brontë
title: Jane Eyre
description: The story of an orphan girl who overcomes her tragic upbringing to become an independent woman.

author: Emily Brontë
title: Wuthering Heights
description: A dark tale of love, passion, and revenge on the Yorkshire moors.

author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
title: Crime and Punishment
description: A poor ex-student in St. Petersburg murders a pawnbroker and wrestles with his ensuing guilt.

author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
title: The Brothers Karamazov
description: A complex philosophical novel exploring faith, free will, and morality through three brothers.

author: Miguel de Cervantes
title: Don Quixote
description: An aging nobleman loses his sanity and decides to become a knight-errant to revive chivalry.

author: Dan Brown
title: The Da Vinci Code
description: A symbologist investigates a murder in the Louvre, uncovering a massive religious conspiracy.

author: Gillian Flynn
title: Gone Girl
description: A psychological thriller surrounding the mysterious disappearance of a man's wife on their anniversary.

author: Stieg Larsson
title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant hacker investigate a decades-old disappearance.

author: Paulo Coelho
title: The Alchemist
description: A young Andalusian shepherd travels to Egypt in search of a hidden treasure.

author: Khaled Hosseini
title: The Kite Runner
description: The heartbreaking story of an unlikely friendship between a wealthy Afghan boy and the son of his father's servant.

author: Yann Martel
title: Life of Pi
description: A young boy survives a shipwreck and shares a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger.

author: Markus Zusak
title: The Book Thief
description: A story narrated by Death about a young girl living in Nazi Germany who steals books.

author: Fredrik Backman
title: A Man Called Ove
description: A curmudgeonly widower finds his life transformed by his new, chatty neighbors.

author: Andy Weir
title: The Martian
description: An astronaut is stranded on Mars and must use his scientific knowledge to survive.

author: Cormac McCarthy
title: The Road
description: A father and son journey across a barren, post-apocalyptic landscape trying to survive.

author: Yuval Noah Harari
title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
description: An exploration of the history and evolution of the human species.

author: Tara Westover
title: Educated
description: A memoir about a woman who grows up in a survivalist family and eventually earns a PhD from Cambridge.

author: Michelle Obama
title: Becoming
description: The deeply personal memoir of the former First Lady of the United States.

author: Daniel Kahneman
title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
description: A psychological exploration of the two systems that drive the way humans think.

author: James Clear
title: Atomic Habits
description: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, incremental changes.

author: Charles Duhigg
title: The Power of Habit
description: An investigation into why habits exist and how they can be changed.

author: Malcolm Gladwell
title: Outliers
description: An examination of the factors that contribute to high levels of success.

author: Susan Cain
title: Quiet
description: A study of introverts and how society undervalues their contributions.

author: Stephen Hawking
title: A Brief History of Time
description: A famous theoretical physicist attempts to explain the mysteries of the universe to non-scientists.

author: Carl Sagan
title: Cosmos
description: A classic exploration of the universe, science, and human history.

author: Patrick Rothfuss
title: The Name of the Wind
description: The autobiography of an adventurer and musician growing up to become a legend.

author: George R.R. Martin
title: A Game of Thrones
description: Noble families fight for control of the Iron Throne in the continent of Westeros.

author: Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman
title: Good Omens
description: An angel and a demon team up to sabotage the impending apocalypse.

author: Alice Walker
title: The Color Purple
description: The story of an African-American woman's life and struggles in the rural South in the early 20th century.

author: Toni Morrison
title: Beloved
description: A powerful novel about an escaped slave who is haunted by the spirit of the child she killed to save from slavery.

author: Margaret Atwood
title: The Handmaid's Tale
description: In a totalitarian, fundamentalist regime, women are subjugated and forced into reproductive roles.

author: Joseph Heller
title: Catch-22
description: A satirical novel about the absurdities of military life and war during World War II.

author: Kurt Vonnegut
title: Slaughterhouse-Five
description: An anti-war sci-fi novel tracking the life of Billy Pilgrim, a man unstuck in time.

author: George Orwell
title: Animal Farm
description: A group of farm animals overthrow their human farmer, only to form a new tyranny.

author: William Golding
title: Lord of the Flies
description: Schoolboys stranded on a deserted island descend into savagery and primitive behavior.
